title: chrysothamnus parryi var. asper
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi var. asper, also known as a variant of the Pygmy Chrysanthemum or Pygmy Desert Chrysanthemum, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The medicinal properties of Chrysothamnus parryi var. asper are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include the relief of symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Chrysothamnus parryi var. asper can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
